result:
Line numbers confirmed. Here's the full trace.

## Syscall number → handler mapping (in-tree, non-generated)

**`arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.tbl:11`**
```
0	common	read			sys_read
```
This hand-maintained table is the source of truth; a build-time script (`syscalltbl.sh`) turns it into the *generated* header `arch/x86/include/generated/asm/syscalls_64.h`, which `arch/x86/entry/syscall_64.c` `#include`s twice (once via `__SYSCALL` as `extern` decls, once to fill `sys_call_table[]`) to build the actual dispatch table — so `sys_call_table[0] == __x64_sys_read`.

## Call chain: hardware entry → `vfs_read`

1. **`arch/x86/entry/entry_64.S:120`** — `entry_SYSCALL_64` (asm) does `call do_syscall_64`
2. **`arch/x86/entry/common.c:73`** — `do_syscall_64(regs, nr)` → calls `do_syscall_x64()` (`common.c:40`)
3. **`arch/x86/entry/common.c:50`** — `do_syscall_x64()` executes `regs->ax = sys_call_table[unr](regs);` — for `nr==0` this is `__x64_sys_read`
4. **`__x64_sys_read`** — generated by the `__X64_SYS_STUBx` macro (`arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:96`), expanded at the `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)` site → **`fs/read_write.c:621`**. Body: `return __se_sys_read(regs->di, regs->si, regs->dx);`
5. **`__se_sys_read`** — generated by `__SYSCALL_DEFINEx` (`arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:228`), same expansion site **`fs/read_write.c:621`** → calls `__do_sys_read(fd, buf, count)` (does sign-extension/pointer checks)
6. **`__do_sys_read`** — this *is* the SYSCALL_DEFINE3 body the developer wrote, at **`fs/read_write.c:621-623`**:
   ```c
   SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, unsigned int, fd, char __user *, buf, size_t, count)
   {
       return ksys_read(fd, buf, count);
   }
   ```
7. **`ksys_read`** — **`fs/read_write.c:602`**, at line **613** calls:
   ```c
   ret = vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos);
   ```
8. **`vfs_read`** — defined at **`fs/read_write.c:450`** (end of chain).

Note: steps 4–5 (`__x64_sys_read`, `__se_sys_read`) are macro-generated named symbols, not separately written source lines — both expand from the single `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)` invocation at `fs/read_write.c:621`, which is why CodeGraph's index doesn't surface them as distinct nodes (macro expansion isn't tracked); I confirmed them by reading `arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h` directly. All other hops (`ksys_read` → `vfs_read`) were also confirmed via `codegraph callees ksys_read`.
